title: Private key material exposure through getEncoded()
problem: The getEncoded() method returns the internal keyBytes array directly instead of a clone. This allows external code to modify the private key material, which is a security vulnerability.

title: Public key coordinates exposed without cloning
problem: The getX() and getY() methods return the internal x and y arrays directly without cloning. This allows external code to modify the public key coordinates.

title: Native library extracted to temp directory without restrictive permissions
problem: The loadLibraryFromResources() method extracts the native library to /tmp/sdf4j-jce-native without setting restrictive file permissions. On multi-user systems, another user could potentially replace the library before it's loaded.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize() which is unreliable
problem: The sessionHandle is only cleaned up in finalize(), which is not guaranteed to run and may run arbitrarily late. This can lead to resource leaks. There should be an explicit close() method or implement AutoCloseable.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize() which is unreliable
problem: The sessionHandle is only cleaned up in finalize(). This class should implement AutoCloseable to provide explicit resource cleanup.

title: Silent truncation of signature values in copyToFixedBuffer
problem: The copyToFixedBuffer function silently truncates data if srcLen > 32 bytes. This could lead to incorrect signature verification or encoding issues without any error indication.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: Like other JCE classes, this class uses finalize() for cleanup. Implementing AutoCloseable would provide better resource management.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: This class uses finalize() for session cleanup. The sessionHandle should be explicitly closable.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: SM2Cipher also uses finalize() for session cleanup.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: SDFSecureRandom uses finalize() for session cleanup. This is particularly problematic since SecureRandom instances are often long-lived.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: SM4KeyGenerator uses finalize() for session cleanup.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: HmacSM3 uses finalize() for session cleanup.

title: Resource cleanup relies on finalize()
problem: SM4Mac uses finalize() for session cleanup.

title: Potential race condition in initialization check
problem: The g_sdf_initialized flag is checked before checking g_device_handle validity, but the flag is set after successful initialization. Between the check and the set, another thread could see an inconsistent state.

title: Memory leak when strdup fails in SetLibraryPath
problem: When GetStringUTFChars succeeds but strdup fails, the code returns without freeing the string from GetStringUTFChars, causing a memory leak.

title: forkCount changed from 0 to 1 may affect test behavior
problem: The forkCount is changed from 0 (no fork, run in Maven process) to 1 (fork new process). The original comment stated this was to "avoid surefire temporary files" and use "system class loader". This change may break tests that relied on the previous behavior.
code:
-                        <!-- 禁用fork，在Maven主进程中运行测试，避免surefire临时文件 -->
-                        <forkCount>0</forkCount>
+                        <forkCount>1</forkCount>
fix: Keep the original forkCount=0 behavior if tests depend on running in the Maven process with system class loader. If the change is intentional, add a comment explaining why the change was made and what the implications are.
